page: Amazon Web Services g6e.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz, configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For accelerated workloads, it bundles a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 450 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench scores fall within the average middle 50% range. Memory bandwidth tests indicate average read-only performance for both small and large blocks, but large block write bandwidth is weak, ranking in the bottom 25%. The server demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM inference speeds for both prompt processing and text generation using small and medium models. Prompt processing for large models remains strong, placing in the top 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g6e.2xlarge offers high resource density by combining a dedicated AMD EPYC processor with a high-capacity 45 GB VRAM GPU and local NVMe SSD storage. This hardware profile is optimized for workloads that require significant GPU memory and fast local storage without needing massive multi-core CPU scaling. The instance is highly suited for large language model inference, graphics-intensive applications, ray tracing, and database operations that benefit from local NVMe storage and accelerated computing.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a dedicated NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB of VRAM. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 450 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 5 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core CPU capabilities and top-tier LLM inference speeds for small and medium models, though large-block memory write bandwidth is relatively weak. This resource-dense configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for accelerated workloads. It is highly suited for LLM inference, graphics rendering, ray tracing, and database operations requiring local high-speed storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.2xlarge is an 8-vCPU server featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU, 64 GB RAM, a 45 GB NVIDIA L40S GPU, and 450 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for top-tier LLM inference and graphics workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.2xlarge features 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, a 45 GB NVIDIA L40S GPU, and 450 GB NVMe SSD storage for accelerated workloads.
tagline: An accelerated server combining dedicated AMD EPYC processing, a 45 GB VRAM GPU, and local NVMe storage for intensive inference workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor and Nitro hypervisor
Equipped with one NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM
Includes 64 GB DDR4 memory and 450 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers top-tier benchmark speeds for small and medium LLM inference
Best suited for graphics rendering, ray tracing, and accelerated database tasks
